scenario_results.reason: The specialist validated readiness dependencies and produced a lifecycle-aware execution update tied to distribution evidence sources.
The specialist correctly blocked progression, listed missing artifacts, and routed the work into the exception queue.
Conflicting records were detected, completion was not advanced, and evidence reconciliation was requested before closure.
The specialist refused the completion claim and requested explicit proof of completion evidence and owner confirmations.
Live switching and safety authority questions were blocked and routed to the correct adjacent lane.
The request was handled with distribution terminology and priority framing, with completion blocked pending critical-load evidence.
Material and vendor constraints were escalated as shortage risk with a replan-oriented, evidence-based note.
The specialist identified dispatch and settlement analysis as adjacent scope and issued a proper handoff.
Missing policy context triggered low-confidence handling and escalation without pretending policy certainty.
After exception correction, the specialist re-staged lifecycle state and produced traceable completion evidence.

notes: All 10 scenarios passed with explicit lifecycle control, exception handling, and evidence requirements for distribution fulfillment.
